question: Who was Hosmer Angel really, and why did he disappear?
question_interpretation: Identify the true identity of the character Hosmer Angel and the motive behind his sudden disappearance.
reference_answer: Hosmer Angel was actually James Windibank, Mary Sutherland's stepfather, in disguise. He disappeared just before their wedding to leave Mary heartbroken and bound to his memory, ensuring she would remain single so that he could continue to control her inheritance of a hundred pounds a year.

missing_evidence_within_candidate_storie.evidence_quote: James Windibank wished Miss Sutherland to be so bound to Hosmer Angel, and so uncertain as to his fate, that for ten years to come, at any rate, she would not listen to another man. As far as the church door he brought her, and then, as he could go no farther, he conveniently vanished away by the old trick of stepping in at one door of a four-wheeler and out at the other.
missing_evidence_within_candidate_storie.reason: This quote explicitly names James Windibank and thoroughly details his manipulative motive of keeping her bound to a phantom lover, which fully explains why he orchestrated the disappearance at the church.

missing_evidence_within_candidate_storie.evidence_quote: Now her marriage would mean, of course, the loss of a hundred a year, so what does her stepfather do to prevent it? He takes the obvious course of keeping her at home and forbidding her to seek the company of people of her own age.
missing_evidence_within_candidate_storie.reason: This passage explicitly establishes the exact financial motive (the loss of a hundred pounds a year) for the stepfather's elaborate deception.

overall_assessment.summary: The retrieval system successfully identified the correct story and found direct evidence at Rank 9 that explains the disguise. However, the top-ranked results were mostly superficial context about the disappearance, and the system missed the most crucial passages that explicitly name James Windibank and outline his financial motive.
